title: The temptation of Jesus part 3
scripture_text: ‘’Then Jesus was led by the Spirit into the wilderness to be tempted by the devil After fasting forty days and forty nights, he was hungry The tempter came to him and said, “If you are the Son of God, tell these stones to become bread.” Jesus answered, “It is written: ‘Man shall not live on bread alone, but on every word that comes from the mouth of God.’’ Then the devil took him to the holy city and had him stand on the highest point of the temple. “If you are the Son of God, he said, “throw yourself down. For it is written: “‘He will command his angels concerning you, and they will lift you up in their hands, so that you will not strike your foot against a stone.’ Jesus answered him, ‘’It is also written: ‘Do not put the Lord your God to the test.’ ’Again, the devil took him to a very high mountain and showed him all the kingdoms of the world and their splendour “All this I will give you,” he said, “if you will bow down and worship me.” ‘Jesus said to him, “Away from me, Satan! For it is written: ‘Worship the Lord your God, and serve him only.’ Then the devil left him, and angels came and attended him.
devotional:
Sometimes being led by the Spirit is uncomfortable.

The Spirit drove Jesus into the wilderness, into a place of desolation. Often we have romantic ideas as to what life would be like if we were led by the Spirit. But sometimes the Holy Spirit will take us to places where we would never choose for ourselves. This particular desert was known as Jeshimmon or "The Desolation." This stretch of land covers a 35×15 mile scarred plot of limestone waste between Jerusalem and the Dead Sea. It remains inhospitable and barren of sustaining human life. Yet, here is where Jesus is sent by God.

What is going on here?

Why did the Spirit drag Jesus into this situation?

What is the point of the temptations?

Jesus was weary and weak; and the very first temptation thrown at Him was designed to take advantage of His diminished condition. But what we learn, of course, is that combating temptation is not a physical exercise, it is a spiritual exercise. Jesus gave every indication of being in a weakened state—a circumstance the devil intended to exploit of course—but in reality, this battle would not be decided by physical endurance, but by spiritual truth and discipline.

Jesus had to learn to be a Son before he could fulfil his role of a servant. When a person has been called and commissioned by God to be involved in his service, it is very tempting to allow that call to redefine a person. So everything we do, or everything we become is filtered through a grid of service. God led his son into the wilderness so that he would learn that being a Son was much more important than being a servant. We can only be true servants when we learn to live as Sons. This has always been God’s way. Often in Christian circles there is a false belief that service is the most important thing about the Christian life, and that God measures our spiritual growth by how much service we do. Although service is an important expression of our love for God, it was never meant to be the purpose of salvation. God longs for intimacy with his children before their service or ministry.
prayer: Father help me be more of a son than a servant.
html:
The temptation of Jesus part 3

Study Text: Matthew 4:1-11
‘’Then Jesus was led by the Spirit into the wilderness to be tempted by the devil After fasting forty days and forty nights, he was hungry The tempter came to him and said, “If you are the Son of God, tell these stones to become bread.” Jesus answered, “It is written: ‘Man shall not live on bread alone, but on every word that comes from the mouth of God.’’ Then the devil took him to the holy city and had him stand on the highest point of the temple. “If you are the Son of God, he said, “throw yourself down. For it is written: “‘He will command his angels concerning you, and they will lift you up in their hands, so that you will not strike your foot against a stone.’ Jesus answered him, ‘’It is also written: ‘Do not put the Lord your God to the test.’ ’Again, the devil took him to a very high mountain and showed him all the kingdoms of the world and their splendour “All this I will give you,” he said, “if you will bow down and worship me.” ‘Jesus said to him, “Away from me, Satan! For it is written: ‘Worship the Lord your God, and serve him only.’ Then the devil left him, and angels came and attended him.

Sometimes being led by the Spirit is uncomfortable.

The Spirit drove Jesus into the wilderness, into a place of desolation. Often we have romantic ideas as to what life would be like if we were led by the Spirit. But sometimes the Holy Spirit will take us to places where we would never choose for ourselves. This particular desert was known as Jeshimmon or "The Desolation." This stretch of land covers a 35×15 mile scarred plot of limestone waste between Jerusalem and the Dead Sea. It remains inhospitable and barren of sustaining human life. Yet, here is where Jesus is sent by God.

What is going on here?

Why did the Spirit drag Jesus into this situation?

What is the point of the temptations?

Jesus was weary and weak; and the very first temptation thrown at Him was designed to take advantage of His diminished condition. But what we learn, of course, is that combating temptation is not a physical exercise, it is a spiritual exercise. Jesus gave every indication of being in a weakened state—a circumstance the devil intended to exploit of course—but in reality, this battle would not be decided by physical endurance, but by spiritual truth and discipline.

Jesus had to learn to be a Son before he could fulfil his role of a servant. When a person has been called and commissioned by God to be involved in his service, it is very tempting to allow that call to redefine a person. So everything we do, or everything we become is filtered through a grid of service. God led his son into the wilderness so that he would learn that being a Son was much more important than being a servant. We can only be true servants when we learn to live as Sons. This has always been God’s way. Often in Christian circles there is a false belief that service is the most important thing about the Christian life, and that God measures our spiritual growth by how much service we do. Although service is an important expression of our love for God, it was never meant to be the purpose of salvation. God longs for intimacy with his children before their service or ministry.

Prayer: Father help me be more of a son than a servant.
Bible in a year reading plan: 2 Samuel 14-16
